example.original_output: Lee fought for the South because he believed in the Union of the states as they were, and he saw the South as a separate, Sovereign nation. He was a Southern man who valued the traditions and way of life of the South, and he didn.ToDateTime the South was being forced to change. He was a loyalist to the South and fought to preserve its way of life.

candidates.text: Lee fought for the South because he believed in the Union of the states as they were, and he viewed the South as a separate, sovereign nation. He was a Southern man who valued the traditions and way of life of the South, and he saw the South being forced to change. He was a loyalist to the South and fought to preserve its way of life.

Lee fought for the South because he believed in the Union of the states as they existed, and he viewed the South as a separate, sovereign nation. He was a Southern man who valued the traditions and way of life of the South and saw the South being forced to change. He was a loyalist to the South and fought to preserve its way of life.

Lee sided with the Confederacy due to his conviction that the states’ existing union should be maintained, and his view of the South as an independent, self-governing nation. A man of the South, he prized Southern customs and lifestyle, and felt the South was being compelled to alter them. His allegiance lay with the South, for which he fought to uphold its established way of life.
